member.name: F:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.AdapterInfo.MaxEncodedSize
member.summary: Upper-bound envelope size for a freshly built adapter info. Caller-supplied destination must be at least this large to avoid truncation.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.AdapterInfo.Encode(System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Encodes this adapter info into a little-endian byte blob. Returns the number of bytes written. Strings that exceed their budget are truncated at a UTF-8 boundary (never in the middle of a code point).

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.CrashDump.ResolveDirectory
member.summary: Resolves the directory into which crash dumps should be written. The env var takes precedence; otherwise falls back to Path.GetTempPath()/etch. The returned directory is created if missing (best-effort).

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.CrashDump.BuildFileName(System.String)
member.summary: Builds the canonical crash-dump filename for the current process using the supplied UTC timestamp string (typically formatted yyyyMMddHHmmssfff). The timestamp is supplied by the caller rather than read from DateTime.UtcNow directly so this type stays free of non-deterministic API calls (see ET0105). Format: crash-<pid>-<utcStamp>.etrp.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.CrashDump.TryWrite(Etch.Abstractions.Diagnostics.ISceneReproducer,System.Int32,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Captures the supplied reproducer and writes the bytes to the resolved crash directory under crash-<pid>-<utcStamp>.etrp. Returns the full path on success, or if the write failed (disk full, permissions, etc.). Never throws — callers typically invoke from a top-level panic handler that must not observe a secondary failure. When is non-null the env var / temp fallback is bypassed — useful for tests that need isolation from the process-global environment.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.GpuSceneReproducer.CalculateUpperBound(System.Int32)
member.summary: Upper-bound envelope size for a capture with the supplied scene-bytes budget. Use when the caller needs to size an ArrayPool rental before calling CaptureTo.

member.name: P:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.ValidationLogRing.TotalWrites
member.summary: Total number of Push calls ever made. If this exceeds Capacity, entries have wrapped.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.ValidationLogRing.Push(Etch.Gpu.ErrorType,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Int64)
member.summary: Records one validation entry. Zero managed allocations on this path — callers typically invoke from the wgpu-native callback thread. The argument is supplied by the caller (typically IFrameClock.NowNanos or Stopwatch.GetTimestamp routed through a determinism seam) so the ring itself stays free of non-deterministic API calls.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Diagnostics.ValidationLogRing.Snapshot
member.summary:
Encodes the ring into a little-endian byte blob for inclusion in an .etrp section. Entries are emitted in chronological order (oldest first). Not called on hot paths.
Blob layout: u32 Count — number of entries actually present (min(TotalWrites, Capacity)) per entry: i64 TimestampTicks u32 ErrorType u16 MessageLength UTF-8 bytes (MessageLength)

member.name: T:Etch.Gpu.Instance
member.summary: A handle to a wgpu-native instance. This is the root of the GPU hierarchy; create one at application startup and dispose at shutdown.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Instance.Create(System.Nullable{Etch.Gpu.Descriptors.InstanceDescriptor})
member.summary: Creates a new wgpu instance with optional capabilities.

member.name: T:Etch.Gpu.Device
member.summary: A handle to a wgpu-native logical device. Created from an ; owns the command queue and is the factory for buffers, textures, pipelines, etc.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Buffer.MapSync(Etch.Gpu.Device,Etch.Gpu.MapMode,System.UInt64,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Synchronously maps the buffer, spinning on until the callback fires or the timeout elapses. Returns true on success.

member.name: T:Etch.Gpu.Validation.ValidationBridge
member.summary: Bridges wgpu-native validation messages into Etch's error infrastructure. In v29 the uncaptured-error callback is set at device-creation time via ; there is no separate wgpuDeviceSetUncapturedErrorCallback setter.

member.name: P:Etch.Gpu.Validation.ValidationBridge.Ring
member.summary: The ring that receives every uncaptured validation message. Thread-safe for a single producer (wgpu callback thread) and any consumer.

member.name: P:Etch.Gpu.Validation.ValidationBridge.TotalDelivered
member.summary: Total number of uncaptured errors that have been delivered to the ring.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Validation.ValidationBridge.AcknowledgeAll
member.summary: Advances the internal counter so that all current ring entries are considered acknowledged. Use in test cleanup to avoid cross-test interference on the shared .

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Validation.ValidationBridge.SetLogger(Etch.IEtchLogger)
member.summary: Attaches the logger used in Release builds. Debug builds panic instead.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Validation.ValidationBridge.ConfigureDeviceDescriptor(Etch.Gpu.Descriptors.DeviceDescriptor*)
member.summary: Fills so that it routes uncaptured errors through . Call this before passing the descriptor to wgpuAdapterRequestDevice.

member.name: M:Etch.Gpu.Validation.ValidationBridge.ThrowIfValidationErrorsPresent(System.String)
member.summary: Throws with code ET-P-0201 GpuValidation if the ring contains any entries written since the last call to this method. Returns the number of new errors found (zero on success).
member.remarks: Use this after Queue.Submit in strict-validation mode (tests).
